step_description: Index coverage analysis on deadlocked tables
rows: Add INCLUDE (status, total, order_id) to eliminate key lookup and reduce lock scope
customer_id, created_date INCLUDE(order_id, status, total, updated_at)
CREATE INDEX IX_Orders_Cust_Date_Covering ON Orders(customer_id, created_date) INCLUDE(order_id, status, total, updated_at)
narrative_hint: The IX_Orders_CustomerID index is missing the columns needed for the SELECT query to be satisfied without a key lookup. The key lookup forces a second lock acquisition on the clustered index row, creating the second lock in the deadlock chain. Adding a covering index eliminates the key lookup, reducing each SELECT to a single index seek with a single row-level S lock rather than two row-level locks. This may not eliminate all deadlocks but will significantly reduce frequency. Combined with READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT, the covering index change provides both a structural fix and an isolation-level fix. Claude should present both recommendations with the RCSI change as the more complete solution.
